blocks.content: Year 6, September 27.
It had already been six years since humanity freed itself from the laws of God.
The fact that there had been no official wars since the beginning of this new era was a miracle that would remain in human history.
Experts called it an obsession with purity.

blocks.speaker: Expert
blocks.content: Who will commit the first crime? Which country will produce the first criminal? That's the issue. No one wants to be remembered as the first villain.
A reporter asked:

blocks.speaker: Reporter
blocks.content: So you're saying humanity hasn't matured—people just don't want to break the illusion of perfection?

blocks.speaker: Expert
blocks.content: Humans are creatures of desire. It's possible crimes are already happening in places we can't see. Some countries may even be suppressing information. Still, I don't think this will last much longer. Once the first official act of evil appears, the second and third will come quickly.
Three years ago, the first official murder occurred.
The perpetrator was a 32-year-old lawyer named Reaper.
What he said to the judge in court was simple:

blocks.speaker: Reaper
blocks.content: I wanted to be the first.
Humans are strange.
Just as the experts predicted, crimes began appearing worldwide—but arrest rates remained notably high.
And so the world continued.
They had come down from 100 percent unified consciousness, but not by all that much...
Back to ordinary life.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I'm heading out!
Amy, having taken off her uniform and wearing a dress for the first time in a while, tripped and fell at the front door.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Puhaha!
Ares burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: You've really perfected the art of falling. Don't go around calling yourself a beauty.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Shut up. This dress is so uncomfortable.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Just wear it however. It's not like you're pretty anyway.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Want to die? How long are you gonna keep lazing around at home? Go get a job or something!

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Nope. Your allowance is more than enough.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You seriously said that to your little sister? Ugh, whatever. I'm leaving. Make sure you eat.
Even while rushing out the door, Amy didn't forget to check her reflection in the mirror.
Ares let out a bitter laugh.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Geez…
He understood his sister's feelings.
Returning to his newspaper, he read a front-page headline.
Yahweh! Flying from the South toward Tormia! Attending his alma mater principal's wedding?!

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Shirone...
Folding the newspaper, Ares looked up at the ceiling.

blocks.speaker: Ares
blocks.content: Amy's going to cry at this rate.
Outside the gates of the magic academy, reporters from all over the world were already gathered.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: This is so embarrassing!
Today's bride, Vice Principal Olivia, grumbled as she tugged at her white wedding dress.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Why? Isn't it nice to be celebrated?
Wearing a tuxedo, Alpheas was calm as ever.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: Are you kidding me? It's bad enough getting married at my age, and people aren't even here for me!

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: It's an honor. One of our school's greatest students is coming back.
Olivia couldn't deny it.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: It's not even certain. Even if he left the South yesterday, there are dozens of countries between there and here.
It was believed that Shirone had returned from the realm of infinite void three years ago.
Believed—because there had been no official announcements, and only a few acquaintances had seen him since.
Alpheas said,

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: We really don't know what it means to return from the infinite void. Maybe it's something deeper than waking from a dream—something like facing pure emptiness.

blocks.speaker: Olivia
blocks.content: He hasn't lost his heart. He's still helping rebuild the world with the Twelve Apostles. Even Vincent says he seems the same.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: And that's what's strange.
If there had been rumors that he'd changed, that would be easier to understand...
What did you see, Shirone? In that true nothingness?
Olina asked,

blocks.speaker: Olina
blocks.content: Do you think he'll stop by home?
Right now, Shirone's birth mother, Ikael, was staying with the Vincent family.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: If he's coming to Tormia, he will. He's never once missed a visit.
Vincent muttered,

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Geez... People talk like my son has to take responsibility for the whole world.
Olina sighed.

blocks.speaker: Olina
blocks.content: I'm more worried about Amy. Those two acted like they couldn't live without each other.

blocks.speaker: Vincent
blocks.content: Maybe...
Ikael thought to herself,

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Everything must have returned to nothing. Dreams, illusions—even emotions. It takes time to rebuild all that. But in the end, he'll remember. He'll remember the most important reason he belongs in this world.
Just like how Goffin once sent Hexa for her.
The South.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Move those supplies to the open lot. This storage building's full!
Kangnan, having returned to her hometown, was helping rebuild southern civilization.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Mister! Don't climb over that—!
Amid the chaos of the construction site, a familiar face appeared.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Huh?
Sein approached with a smile.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Wow! It's been so long! Has it been a year? What brings you to the South all of a sudden?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Something came up. Just thought I'd check in.
They moved to a quiet clearing and sat on some lumber, sipping drinks.
Sein asked,

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: How's Shirone?

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: He left last night. Blitz said he'd fly him there, so he should make it in time.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Has he made a decision?

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: I don't know. He's hard to read. But if he's going to the wedding, that means he intends to see her.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Yeah...
Sein asked again,

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: What about you? Are you okay?
Kangnan understood what he meant.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: I'm okay. Gaold isn't in pain anymore. That's enough for me. What about you? Are you in touch with Miro?

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: No. I'm not avoiding her or anything, though. Miro is finally living her own life—not as some divine guardian, just as a woman. At least for now, I don't want her to feel responsible for anything.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: Yeah...
In the end, their feelings were the same.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: You're living a beautiful love, aren't you?
Sein said with a smile.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Maybe.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Go.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: I'm not going.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Go!

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: I said I'm not going, damn it! Why are there so many events? Now I finally understand what Shirone went through.
As key figures in world peace, the two traveled from country to country attending ceremonies and events.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Still, this time the event fee's pretty generous. We don't have a single coin saved, you know!

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Don't get too caught up in the world.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Look who's talking. It's because of all the trouble you caused. What the hell did you do for the Magic Association to blacklist you?
Gaold grumbled in a teasing tone.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: I was busy saving you.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Agh, seriously!
Frustrated, Miro started swinging her arms, jabbing Gaold in the ribs.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Ah! That was a bone—definitely a bone!
Grimacing in pain, Gaold collapsed onto the bed, prompting a sigh from Miro.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Why are you such a crybaby? What a waste of a body. Why do you even work out?

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Heh, you know why.
Gaold, now lying on his back, spread his arms and winked.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Still got some energy left, right?
Miro glanced at the clock for a moment.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Only 30 minutes.

blocks.speaker: Unnamed Character
blocks.content: Amy!
When they arrived at Alpheas Magic School where the wedding was being held, she spotted familiar faces.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Tess! Seriel!
Rian and Fermi were following close behind.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: It's been so long! Sorry, I've been caught up with promotion exams. How've you been?

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Haha! Same here. You're here for the wedding?
Though Seriel and Fermi had ties to the school, Rian and Tess didn't.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Of course! Shirone's coming, right? Just let him show up—I'm gonna deck him.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: Come on, it's just a rumor.
As they entered the venue, they ran into more familiar faces among the large crowd.
Canis and Arin, Maya and Kayden, Dante and Plu were also waiting in the guest seats.
And then…

blocks.speaker: Iruki or Nade
blocks.content: Amy!
Always a source of laughter, but also someone who brought too many memories with him—

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Iruki! Nade!
Two of the Three Idiots walked over.
When Amy saw the baby in Nade's arms, her eyes widened.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No way?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Haha! Yep. She finally made it into the world. Daisy, say hi. This is your Aunt Amy.
Amy burst into laughter.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Daisy? A girl?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Yeah. Cute, right? Though to be honest, she's still so new we don't really know yet.
Iruki added,

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: It's a fifty-fifty chance. If she wants to take after Liz, she'll have to fight a tough battle with herself.
Nade retorted,

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: If she's in a tough battle, then what about you?
Amy smiled warmly.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: They haven't changed at all.
When the rehearsal began, a crowd of reporters gathered.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: There are so many.
Knowing who they were waiting for, Amy's heart began to pound.
A shadow passed over the outdoor garden.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hey, is that…?
A massive dragon glowing with blue light glided through the sky and landed in the distance.
Then a voice rang out.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thanks, Blitz.
Blitz bowed politely and flew away again. A figure in a hood was left standing.
Shirone turned around with a smile on his face.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Hey.
Camera flashes exploded like fireworks, and tears welled up in Nade's eyes.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Shi…
All their friends rushed toward him at once.

blocks.speaker: Friends
blocks.content: Shirone!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Haha! You guys been well?

blocks.speaker: Friend
blocks.content: You jerk! How could you be so busy you can't even write a single letter?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sorry. Things have been… huh?
Shirone's face lit up.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Nade! You?

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Yeah! What do you think? I created life before you. Daisy, meet Uncle Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Daisy
blocks.content: Hi?
Shirone lifted her up, and Daisy gave him a bright smile.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Haha! Seems like she likes you. Just as I thought—she's my daughter, she's got good instincts.
While everyone chatted excitedly, Amy stood watching from a distance.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What the heck? I thought something serious had happened…

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: He's exactly the same.
Feeling a bit of disappointment, Amy turned away just as Tess walked up to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: What are you doing? Go see Amy. Don't tell me you forgot everything in that infinite void or whatever?
Shirone gave a bittersweet smile but didn't answer.
The wedding started a little late as the school staff cleared the reporters.
During her speech, Olivia mentioned Alpheas's late wife, Erina.
She spoke of her gratitude, her regrets, and the responsibilities she would carry until the end of her life.
Alpheas added briefly:

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: That's love in old age.
The guests burst into laughter, and the ceremony ended in a wave of joyful blessings.
And during the reception, for the first time in six years—Shirone and Amy met again.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Amy.
As if by some unspoken cue, the hall fell silent and everyone turned to look at them.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: It's been a while.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmph, now you show up? Do you know this wedding felt longer than the six years I waited for you?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm sorry. I needed time—time to be sure. To know if what I was thinking, what I was feeling… was really right.
Amy asked gently, concern in her eyes.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: What happened?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: In the world of Infinite Nothingness…
Shirone fell silent for a long time, but no one dared to even breathe too loudly. They waited.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I didn't exist. Because I wasn't there, there was nothing. No way to explain it either. I simply… didn't exist. No time, no space, not a single thing.
A chill swept through the crowd.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It must have been terrifying. But maybe it's because there was no fear at all—that it was truly, perfectly nothing. And then… one moment, I existed again. In this world.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I see.
What kind of heart must he have had, realizing only after existing again that he had been in absolute nothingness?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even now, I don't know. I don't know what happened there that allowed me to return here. I believe it was to meet you… but I had to be certain. Otherwise, the vast fear of that nothingness would swallow me up again, and I'd lose my sense of self. So…

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You traveled the world?
Shirone nodded.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I worked for humanity, I took care of my family, I carried out every piece of my vision. One by one, I resolved everything… so I could meet you.
That's how he spent those years.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And now, I'm sure. The reason I returned from the Infinite Nothingness is...
With all the courage in his heart, Shirone said:

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I love you.
All the illusions he had wandered through in a world without time, space, or even himself—

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: You…
—became complete certainty in this moment, and brought a smile to his face.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: You are my infinity.
In a world that felt frozen in time, Amy's quiet sobs began to break through.
Hhkk... huhuhh...
Tess watched her kindly as Amy nodded through her tears, wiping her eyes with both hands.

blocks.speaker: Tess
blocks.content: I'm happy for you, Amy.
As everyone looked on with warmth in their eyes, Nade clapped and called out:

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Alright, alright! Let's get this moment on record! We won't get another chance like this until Iruki's wedding—and let's face it, that's never happening.

blocks.speaker: Friends
blocks.content: Puahaha!
Their friends gathered one by one as the same optical camera used for the wedding photos was set up again.
Amy, now smiling again, turned to Shirone before running over to join them.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I want to be in it too!

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Good. You stand in the middle. Fermi, you go over there. Shirone, what are you doing? Hurry up!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Got it.
Looking at the baby giggling between her mom and dad, Shirone thought:

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Someday, we'll travel the stars.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: When I count to three, say 'Daisy' together, okay? You know how this works?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: We'll learn what truly matters from the secrets of the stars.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Alright, alright. It's cramped! Hurry up.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: On the day we overcome division and conflict and reach an integrated mental system...

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Okay, gather in. Taking the shot now!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: ...we'll be dreaming of another universe, from the edge of this one.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Here we go! One, two, three!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: And so, we will endlessly, eternally—

blocks.speaker: All
blocks.content: Daisy!

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: —journey into infinity.
